evidence.snippet: Specim airborne hyperspectral systems combine lightweight cameras, robust spectral calibration, and optimized optics for drones and aircraft.

![Specim AFX10 Hyperspectral Camera For Drones](https://www.specim.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/Products-Specim-AFX10-2.jpg "Specim AFX10 Hyperspectral Camera For Drones")
**Specim AFX10**
VNIR (400–1000 nm)
[](https://www.specim.com/products/specim-afx17/)
![Specim AFX17 Hyperspectral Camera For Drones](https://www.specim.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Products-Specim-AFX17-2.jpg "Specim AFX17 Hyperspectral Camera For Drones")
**Specim AFX17**
NIR (900–1700 nm)

items.notes: Specim makes hyperspectral imaging cameras explicitly designed for drone/UAV mounting (AFX series, AisaFENIX). These are EO sensors used as airborne payloads for remote sensing.

products_categories.notes: Specim's core business is hyperspectral imaging cameras. Their AFX/AisaFENIX lines are specifically for drone/aircraft use. Industrial FX cameras also sold to OEMs and integrators for machine vision but primary UAS-relevant category is isr_payloads.

evidence.snippet: Specim was founded in 1995 and has been a part of the Konica Minolta Group since 2020. We operate globally. With headquarters in Oulu, Finland, sales offices in the US, China, Germany, and Spain, and an extensive distributor network, we can serve our customers locally and worldwide.
Specim airborne hyperspectral systems combine lightweight cameras, robust spectral calibration, and optimized optics for drones and aircraft.

World-leading hyperspectral imaging cameras and systems
for industry, OEM, integrators, research & airborne users

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Specim designs and manufactures hyperspectral camera sensors for drone mounting. Their AFX10/AFX17 cameras are components that integrate into third-party UAV platforms. They explicitly target OEM builders and system integrators.

items.name: SisuROCK Workstation
items.product_type: Automated hyperspectral imaging system for geology/drill cores

evidence.snippet:
![SisuROCK Workstation](https://www.specim.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Products-SisuROCK-3.jpg "SisuROCK Workstation")
**SisuROCK Workstation**
**SisuROCK Workstation**
A fully automated hyperspectral imaging system that integrates hyperspectral cameras from visible to thermal range, an RGB camera, and a 3D imager to rapidly scan drill cores and geological samples for precise mineral identification and compositional analysis.

products.notes: Many more product SKUs exist (FX50, FX120, GX17, FX19, SX25, Fenix, ImSpector spectrographs, Lumo/CaliGeo software, accessories) but airborne/drone-relevant products captured above.

evidence.snippet: Specim is the leading global supplier of hyperspectral imaging solutions.

readiness.notes: Specim offers a broad catalog of named SKUs sold to OEMs and integrators worldwide, ISO 9001 certified. No custom/prototype-only posture; serial production of camera lines.

unresolved_questions: NDAA Section 848/889 compliance status not disclosed on site (Finnish company, Konica Minolta subsidiary)
Blue UAS cleared list status not disclosed

fetch_requests.source_hint: guessed from site nav — linked as 'Airborne systems' in product menu of all fetched pages

tagline: Finnish manufacturer of hyperspectral imaging cameras spanning VNIR through LWIR (400 nm–12.3 μm), with airborne systems (AisaFENIX series) and compact drone payloads (AFX10/AFX17). Production-ready across industrial line-scan, laboratory, and field variants. NDAA status and U.S. defense integration unknown; public evidence does not indicate Blue UAS certification or named U.S. military programs.
